what_it_does: A vanilla-JS presentation layer that turns normalized unit, story, piece, and decision data into a phone-screen-like DOM element. It defines pure data-to-DOM renderers, a V-corner verb wheel with tap interaction, deterministic text cleanup from raw digests, source adapters for corpus records and story nodes, and async gallery/decision mounting helpers that fetch data and emit CustomEvents for host integration.
observations: Attaches to a global `DNA` namespace via an IIFE.
Embeds inline SVG for the VI logo and a 24px line-icon set.
Uses `data-anchorable` and `data-address` attributes for directable targets.
Degrades gracefully: failed resolves render a legible 'unresolved' unit instead of throwing.
Separates pure rendering from impure binding and async integration.
standouts: The 'one renderer, many sources' design: corpus records, authored story nodes, and decision cards all normalize into the same presentation path.
The V-corner 90-degree socket wheel with CSS-driven collapse/expand and host-wired verbs.
The deterministic `voiceFromDigest` floor that turns raw filenames/digests into human-readable titles and card lines.
Inline icon language and VI SVG themable via currentColor.
conventions: Uses an IIFE and a global `DNA` object for namespacing.
Pure render functions take data and return an element; interaction is opt-in via `DNA.bindVee`.
CSS classes and tokens are reused from `phone.css`; no bespoke values in renderers.
Uses a small `esc` helper for HTML escaping.
Defensive checks with `typeof` before calling optional `DNA.*` helpers.
CustomEvents carry detail objects for host wiring.
Async functions degrade cleanly on fetch failure.
concerns: Heavy reliance on global `DNA` and `window` for cross-module coordination.
Builds HTML via `innerHTML` and string concatenation, even with escaping.
Hardcoded geometry constants (`PVx`, `PVy`, `R`) for the V-corner socket positions.
Some optional dependencies (`DNA.org.hubNetwork`, `DNA.renderArchetype`, `DNA.decisionDevice`) are assumed but not guaranteed.
notes: This file is the visual face of the unit: it renders the phone-screen archetype, wires the V-corner verb wheel, and leaves data resolution and real verb behavior to the host/integration layers.
questions: What is the exact return type/contract for `DNA.org.hubNetwork`?
How are `DNA.renderArchetype`, `DNA.decisionDevice`, `DNA.renderExplained`, and `DNA.decisionSlide` expected to be loaded into the host?
What is the intended behavior when `DNA._decisionArchetype` is missing in `renderDecisionGallery`?
What is the current usage of `DNA.renderPiece` relative to `renderUnit`?
How is the sixth 'generate' socket intended to be added to `DNA.VEE_SOCKETS`?
purpose_vs_actual: The stated purpose as the live-compose presentation rung for normalized units matches the code. It also covers decision-card integration and a second piece archetype, which is slightly broader but still within the presentation layer.
apparent_themes: Pure rendering separated from impure binding and integration.
intention_signals: The file is trying to create a reusable, source-agnostic visual substrate so that corpus records, authored story nodes, and decision cards can share one consistent phone-screen presentation language, while exposing a discoverable V-corner verb wheel and a clean deterministic text floor when richer AI voice is unavailable.
novelty: The V-corner geometry with CSS-collapsed sockets that fan out on a 90-degree arc, combined with event-driven host wiring for socket verbs, and a deterministic digest-to-voice transform that acts as a baseline human-readable renderer.
contribution: A self-contained, framework-free DOM renderer and interaction binder that normalizes heterogeneous source records into a consistent phone-screen presentation, provides deterministic text cleanup, and emits integration events so hosts can wire data resolution and verb behavior separately.
summary_for_embedding: unit-view.js is a vanilla-JS presentation layer that renders normalized unit, story, piece, and decision data into a phone-screen DOM archetype, binds a V-corner verb wheel, and provides async gallery/decision mounting helpers.
intention_for_embedding: To give the counterpart-design surface a single, reusable, source-agnostic visual renderer with honest degradation and live host integration via CustomEvents.

symbols.does: Toggles the open/closed CSS state of the V-corner wheel and the screen root.
symbols.description: `setOpen` receives a boolean and applies the `open` class to the `.uv-vee` element and the `vee-open` class to the screen root. This lets CSS animate the 90-degree socket sweep and lets stat-chips recede behind an open wheel.

symbols.does: Closes the V-corner wheel by delegating to `setOpen(false)`.
symbols.description: `close` is a convenience closure used by tap-away and socket-fire handlers to collapse the V-corner wheel after an interaction.

symbols.does: Title-cases a slug-like string into a readable label.
symbols.description: `_tc` replaces hyphens and underscores with spaces, then capitalizes each word. It is used by `voiceFromDigest` to turn filenames and matched terms into clean headline words.

symbols.does: Maps a filename to a short editorial role label for neighbour nodes.
symbols.description: `roleOf` strips the extension and returns labels like `entry`, `test`, `types`, `cli`, or the first segment of the basename. It provides a best-effort role for the constellation view until a richer voice transform is available.

symbols.does: Normalizes a recall score into a visible weight range.
symbols.description: `norm` maps the minimum-to-maximum score spread in a neighbour set to the interval [0.3, 1.0], so the resulting node sizes in the constellation reflect relative recall without collapsing weak links to zero.
